verses.text: 'Arise, shine; for thy light is come, and the glory of the LORD is risen upon thee'; the Gentiles shall come to thy light. Darkness gives place to glory. The coming of God's light draws the nations to His people.

'Arise, shine; for thy light is come, and the glory of the LORD is risen upon thee'; the Gentiles shall come to thy light. Darkness gives place to glory. The coming of God's light draws the nations to His people.

'Arise, shine; for thy light is come, and the glory of the LORD is risen upon thee'; the Gentiles shall come to thy light. Darkness gives place to glory. The coming of God's light draws the nations to His people.
'Lift up thine eyes': sons come from far, daughters are carried, and the wealth of the sea flows in. The scattered are gathered. The joy of restoration swells the heart of Zion.
'Lift up thine eyes': sons come from far, daughters are carried, and the wealth of the sea flows in. The scattered are gathered. The joy of restoration swells the heart of Zion.
Camels of Midian, the gold and incense of Sheba, and the ships of Tarshish bring tribute to the LORD. The nations honour God. The riches once given to idols are devoted to His glory.
Camels of Midian, the gold and incense of Sheba, and the ships of Tarshish bring tribute to the LORD. The nations honour God. The riches once given to idols are devoted to His glory.
Camels of Midian, the gold and incense of Sheba, and the ships of Tarshish bring tribute to the LORD. The nations honour God. The riches once given to idols are devoted to His glory.
Camels of Midian, the gold and incense of Sheba, and the ships of Tarshish bring tribute to the LORD. The nations honour God. The riches once given to idols are devoted to His glory.

Strangers build her walls, her gates stand open continually, for the nation that will not serve her shall perish. Former enemies now serve. God turns the world's hostility into willing service to His city.

Strangers build her walls, her gates stand open continually, for the nation that will not serve her shall perish. Former enemies now serve. God turns the world's hostility into willing service to His city.

Strangers build her walls, her gates stand open continually, for the nation that will not serve her shall perish. Former enemies now serve. God turns the world's hostility into willing service to His city.
The glory of Lebanon adorns His sanctuary, and former oppressors bow, calling her 'The city of the LORD.' Beauty and honour are restored. Those who despised God's people shall yet revere them.
The glory of Lebanon adorns His sanctuary, and former oppressors bow, calling her 'The city of the LORD.' Beauty and honour are restored. Those who despised God's people shall yet revere them.
Once forsaken, she becomes 'an eternal excellency,' her brass turned to gold, her officers peace. Reversal is complete. God exchanges His people's shame for lasting glory.
Once forsaken, she becomes 'an eternal excellency,' her brass turned to gold, her officers peace. Reversal is complete. God exchanges His people's shame for lasting glory.
Once forsaken, she becomes 'an eternal excellency,' her brass turned to gold, her officers peace. Reversal is complete. God exchanges His people's shame for lasting glory.

Violence shall cease; her walls shall be called Salvation; 'the LORD shall be unto thee an everlasting light,' her sun no more to set. God Himself is her light. In the city of God sorrow and darkness end for ever.

Violence shall cease; her walls shall be called Salvation; 'the LORD shall be unto thee an everlasting light,' her sun no more to set. God Himself is her light. In the city of God sorrow and darkness end for ever.

Violence shall cease; her walls shall be called Salvation; 'the LORD shall be unto thee an everlasting light,' her sun no more to set. God Himself is her light. In the city of God sorrow and darkness end for ever.

Her people shall be 'all righteous,' inheriting the land, the small one becoming a thousand; 'I the LORD will hasten it.' Growth and holiness are sure. God will swiftly fulfil all He has promised His people.
